diagnosis: Root cause: The reward function provides only positive per-step rewards (alive_bonus + progress_reward) with no penalties for dangerous states (high speed, tilt, crashes) and a sparse terminal bonus. This gives the agent no incentive to avoid crashes, and the constant alive_bonus offers no differential learning signal. Consequently, the agent learns to terminate early (crash) because longer episodes with low progress yield only slightly higher total reward, and the terminal bonus is too rare to shape behavior during early training.

component_verdicts.reason: Constant positive reward (0.5) provides no state-dependent gradient; agent cannot learn to improve survival.
Linear in progress gives weak signal early on; needs higher weight or combination with directional shaping to effectively guide agent toward pad.
Large bonus (200) for success is correctly motivating, but too sparse; requires intermediate shaping to avoid early termination.
